contents.value: **module** — SystemVerilog keyword.
**endmodule** — SystemVerilog keyword.
**interface** — SystemVerilog keyword.
**endinterface** — SystemVerilog keyword.
**class** — SystemVerilog keyword.
**$display** — Built-in function/command in SystemVerilog.
**$write** — Built-in function/command in SystemVerilog.
**$monitor** — Built-in function/command in SystemVerilog.
**$strobe** — Built-in function/command in SystemVerilog.
**$finish** — Built-in function/command in SystemVerilog.

A **pointer** stores the memory address of another variable.
```c
int *ptr = &value;
```
A **struct** groups related data fields into a single composite type.
An **enum** defines a type with a fixed set of named constants.
A **union** stores different data types in the same memory location.

**typedef** creates an alias for an existing type.
```c
typedef unsigned long ulong;
```

**malloc** allocates a block of memory on the heap and returns a pointer.
```c
int *p = malloc(n * sizeof(int));
```

**free** releases previously allocated heap memory.
```c
free(ptr);
```
**sizeof** returns the size in bytes of a type or variable.
**volatile** tells the compiler a variable may change unexpectedly (e.g., hardware register).
**static** limits scope to the current file/function, or preserves value between calls.
**extern** declares a variable or function defined in another translation unit.
**inline** suggests the compiler insert the function body at the call site.
**const** marks a value as read-only after initialization.
**register** hints the compiler to store the variable in a CPU register.

**Preprocessor directives** run before compilation.
```c
#include, #define, #ifdef, #pragma
```

```systemverilog
logic [signed] [width-1:0] name
```
Four-state data type (0, 1, x, z) that can be used in both procedural assignments and continuous assignments, replacing the need to choose between reg and wire in Verilog.

```systemverilog
wire [signed] [width-1:0] name
```
Net type representing a physical connection driven by continuous assignments or module port connections, resolving multiple drivers according to resolution functions in hardware modeling.

```systemverilog
reg [signed] [width-1:0] name
```
Variable type that holds its value between procedural assignments in always blocks, representing storage elements in sequential logic though not necessarily synthesized as registers.

```systemverilog
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin ... end
```
Procedural block specifically for modeling flip-flop based sequential logic, enforced by synthesis tools to contain only synchronous assignments with clock and optional reset sensitivity.

```systemverilog
always_comb begin ... end
```
Procedural block for modeling combinational logic that automatically infers a complete sensitivity list from all signals read within the block, preventing unintended latch inference.

```systemverilog
always_latch begin ... end
```
Procedural block explicitly intended for latch-based logic, with automatic sensitivity list inference and tool checks ensuring the block correctly implements level-sensitive storage elements.

```systemverilog
constraint name { expression1; expression2; ... }
```
Defines declarative constraints on random variables within a class, specifying valid value ranges, relationships, distributions, and conditional rules for constrained random verification.

```systemverilog
covergroup name @(event); coverpoint var { bins name = {values}; } endgroup
```
Defines a functional coverage model with coverpoints and cross coverage for tracking which design scenarios have been exercised during simulation verification campaigns.

```systemverilog
assert property (@(posedge clk) antecedent |-> consequent) else $error("msg")
```
SystemVerilog assertion that formally specifies and checks temporal properties of the design during simulation, using sequences and properties for protocol and timing verification.

```systemverilog
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```systemverilog
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```systemverilog
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```systemverilog
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```systemverilog
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```systemverilog
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```systemverilog
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```systemverilog
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.
